task_completion.description: Agent opens the rasterizer source code files in a text editor or IDE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE (like VS Code, Vim, or Nano) is open, displaying Python files from the /home/ga/workspace/tiny_rasterizer/ directory (e.g., rasterizer.py, geometry.py, camera.py, or render.py).

task_completion.description: Agent modifies the mathematical or logical code within the rendering pipeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows active changes, additions, or deletions to the code handling backface culling, Z-buffer depth testing, perspective divide, barycentric coordinates, or viewport transformation.

task_completion.description: Agent executes the render script to generate the 3D image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows the command `python render.py` being executed.

task_completion.description: The output image file is successfully generated in the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal or file explorer shows the creation or updating of an output image file (e.g., output.png) after the render script is executed.

task_completion.description: The generated image displays a correct, undistorted 3D render of a torus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image viewer is open showing the final render. The image must depict a solid 3D torus (donut shape) that is right-side up, has correct depth sorting (closer parts occlude farther parts, not inside-out), and lacks geometric distortion.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the Python source code to fix the bugs rather than downloading or copying a pre-rendered image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with and editing the Python source files before running the render script.

integrity.description: The agent generated the image by running the provided script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the actual execution of `python render.py` resulting in the image creation, rather than the agent renaming an existing file or mocking the output.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided lightweight Python software rasterizer without importing heavy external 3D rendering engines.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code visible in the editor does not show new imports for external rendering libraries like PyOpenGL, Blender (bpy), or matplotlib 3D toolkits used to bypass the manual rasterization process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 5 specific mathematical bugs in the rasterizer: barycentric coordinate miscalculation, perspective divide failure on Z, Y-axis viewport inversion, reversed winding order in normal calculation, and reversed z-buffer depth testing. The final output must be a successfully regenerated output.png file showing a correct 3D torus. A passing score requires fixing at least 3 out of 5 bugs (score >= 60).
